brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: Bronze ingest of the Anthropic Economic Index release_2025_03_27 into raw.anthropic_economic_index. Three source CSVs are LEFT JOINed on normalized task text (case-insensitive, trailing period stripped): task_pct_v2.csv (3,365 tasks, column pct is global share in 0-100 percent units summing to 100.0), automation_vs_augmentation_by_task.csv (3,364 rows, 5 interaction-mode fractions + filtered summing to 1.0 per row), and onet_task_statements.csv (19,530 rows acting as the SOC bridge since the 2025-03-27 release does not ship onet_task_mappings.csv). Grain is composite (task_id, soc_code) because task-to-SOC is many-to-many (max 34-way fan-out). For each source task mapping to N distinct SOCs, the ingestor emits N Bronze rows with task_pct = raw_pct / N so SUM(task_pct) across all Bronze rows is preserved at ~100 (global-share invariant). The task_name='none' placeholder is kept with soc_code=NULL and unsplit task_pct (1 row) so downstream can see the uncategorized share; Silver filters it out. Automation collapsed via Anthropic v2 methodology: automation_pct = (directive + feedback_loop) * 100; augmentation_pct = (task_iteration + validation + learning) * 100 (learning counts as augmentation because Claude is assisting the user). When filtered >= 0.999 both fields emit None. Observed output row count: 4,082 (4,081 SOC-matched + 1 placeholder), within the 3,800-4,400 tolerance band.

documentation.description: Bronze ingestor for the Anthropic Economic Index. Reads three release CSVs, joins them on normalized task text, emits one row per (O*NET task, SOC) pair with task_pct split across the fan-out to preserve the global 100% share invariant. Applies Anthropic v2 automation/augmentation collapse rules.

fields.name: pct
fields.description: Global share of classified Claude conversations, 0-100 percent units; column sum = 100.0

fields.name: feedback_loop
fields.description: Fraction of conversations where Claude acts under user feedback

fields.name: directive
fields.description: Fraction where Claude acts directly on user instruction

fields.name: task_iteration
fields.description: Fraction where Claude iterates on user work

fields.name: validation
fields.description: Fraction where Claude validates user work

fields.name: learning
fields.description: Fraction where user learns from Claude (augmentation)

inputDatasetFacets.description: Used as the task-to-SOC bridge since release_2025_03_27 does not ship onet_task_mappings.csv. Normalized task text is the join key; many-to-many mapping (max 34 distinct SOCs per task).

fields.name: O*NET-SOC Code
fields.description: Detailed O*NET-SOC code XX-XXXX.NN; overlay suffix stripped to get canonical SOC

fields.name: task_id
fields.transformation: DIRECT from onet_task_statements.Task ID; part of composite grain (not unique alone)

fields.name: soc_code
fields.transformation: DERIVED: onet_task_statements.O*NET-SOC Code normalized to XX-XXXX (overlay .NN suffix stripped); NULL for task_name='none' placeholder

fields.name: task_pct
fields.transformation: DERIVED: task_pct_v2.pct / n_soc_per_task (N-way split across fan-out); preserves global 100% invariant

fields.name: automation_pct
fields.transformation: DERIVED: (directive + feedback_loop) * 100 from automation_vs_augmentation_by_task; None when filtered >= 0.999

fields.name: augmentation_pct
fields.transformation: DERIVED: (task_iteration + validation + learning) * 100 from automation_vs_augmentation_by_task; None when filtered >= 0.999

fields.name: source_release
fields.transformation: FRAMEWORK: release directory name, e.g. release_2025_03_27

task_id.transformationDescription: Direct from onet_task_statements.Task ID. When a task_pct row has no O*NET bridge match (e.g. task_name='none'), task_id falls back to the normalized task text. Part of composite grain (task_id, soc_code); task_id alone is NOT unique because a task fanning out to N SOCs produces N rows sharing task_id.

task_statement.transformationDescription: Direct copy of the task_name text from task_pct_v2 (trimmed, sentinel-coerced). The normalized form (lowercased, trailing period stripped, whitespace collapsed) is used as the join key across the three CSVs but the stored value preserves original casing.

soc_code.transformationDescription: Normalized from the O*NET overlay form XX-XXXX.NN to canonical SOC XX-XXXX (overlay suffix stripped at the dot). Validated against the ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ regex. Dash-less 6-digit strings are recovered by inserting a dash after position 2. Malformed SOC values cause the (task, SOC) mapping to be dropped; if ALL of a task's SOC mappings are malformed the task is dropped entirely (NOT emitted as a NULL-SOC row) to avoid inflating the NULL-SOC count beyond the single expected placeholder row. Nullable: the task_name='none' placeholder row emits soc_code=NULL by design.

soc_title.transformationDescription: Direct from onet_task_statements.Title. First non-null title per (task, SOC) pair wins.

task_pct.transformationDescription: N-way fan-out split: task_pct = raw_pct / n_soc_per_task where n_soc_per_task is the number of distinct SOCs the task maps to in the O*NET bridge. Units: 0-100 percent (global share). The split preserves the P0 invariant SUM(task_pct) across all Bronze rows ~ 100 regardless of fan-out. The task_name='none' placeholder is NOT split (its pct is unchanged) because it has no SOC mapping.

automation_pct.transformationDescription: Anthropic v2 methodology: automation_pct = (directive + feedback_loop) * 100. Represents the share of a task's conversations where Claude acts directly (vs. assisting). Emits None when filtered >= 0.999 (entire task excluded by safety filters; no usable signal).

augmentation_pct.transformationDescription: Anthropic v2 methodology: augmentation_pct = (task_iteration + validation + learning) * 100. The `learning` axis counts as augmentation because it represents user-learns-from-Claude (Claude is assisting, not acting). Emits None when filtered >= 0.999.

source_release.transformationDescription: Framework-injected release directory name (e.g. release_2025_03_27). Used downstream as the data-vintage pointer for the LICENSE_SOURCES.md citation and for Silver's anthropic_source_release field.

ingested_at.transformationDescription: Framework-injected UTC timestamp at ingest time.

source_method.transformationDescription: Framework-injected: hf_git_clone (live clone) or local_cache (offline fallback).

rowCountLineage.description: 3,365 source tasks in task_pct_v2.csv -> 4,082 Bronze rows. Breakdown: 1 task_name='none' placeholder emits 1 NULL-SOC row. Of the remaining 3,364 tasks, 82 fan out to 2-34 distinct SOCs; summing across the fan-out yields 4,081 SOC-matched (task, SOC) pairs. Net: 4,081 + 1 = 4,082 rows, within the 3,800-4,400 tolerance band.
